title: Political Precept.
content:
All that thou doest is right; but, friend, don't carry this precept
    On too far, be content, all that is right to effect.
    It is enough to true zeal, if what is existing be perfect;
    False zeal always would find finished perfection at once.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Friedrich Schiller, titled "Political Precept.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
